gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (67/ 9)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (67/ 9)
text: إِذَا صَافَحَ الرَّجُلُ صَاحِبَهُ فَالَّذِي يَلْزَمُ التَّصَافُحَ أَعْظَمُ أَجْراً مِنَ الَّذِي يَدَعُ أَلَا وَ إِنَّ الذُّنُوبَ لَتَتَحَاتُّ فِيمَا بَيْنَهُمْ حَتَّى لَا يَبْقَى ذَنْبٌ .

en.hubeali: From him, from his father, from the one who narrated it, from Zayd Bin Al jahm Al Hilaly, from Malik Bin Ayn,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘When the man shakes the hand of his companions, so the one who necessitated the handshake would have greater Recompense than the one who was invited (to it). Indeed! And the sins would erode during what is between them until there does not remain a sin’.
en.sarwar: 13. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from his father from those who narrated to him from Zayd ibn al-Jahm al-Hilali from Malik ibn ‘Ayun from abu Ja’far (a.s.) who has said the following: “When two people shake hands, the one who continues holding his hand will receive greater reward than the one who releases early. However, the sins fall off of them until no sins remain.”
